images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of our dataset and evaluation pipeline, covering six content categories and six generation/editing tasks. Model outputs are assessed by both human annotators (explainable schema) and vision–language models (scores only).
Figure 2:Illustrative examples from our dataset, showing successful and failure cases for each task

Figure 3:Examples include object-level issues, where expected objects are missing or distorted, and segment-level issues, where SoM partitions highlight specific regions with visual inconsistencies that affect evaluation scores.
Figure 4:Mean human evaluation scores across metrics by topic (left) and task (right).
Figure 5:Overall human rating by task and topic for the four unified models that support all six tasks.
Figure 6:Percentage of cases where the model generating completely new image or simply return input in image editing tasks.
Figure 7:Label Studio interface: annotators read the prompt, inspect the image, rate four criteria (1–5), and flag VLM/SoM-suggested object- and segment-level issues.
Figure 8:Instruction-following problem: The model placed red and green crewmates instead of pink and yellow, and the yellow sign’s position does not match the request.
Figure 11:Examples of generating a new image when the task is editing.
Figure 12:Examples of plots and diagram issues.

Figure 14:Distribution of error pixel proportions across the dataset. Histograms illustrate the fraction of error pixels relative to total mask pixels, excluding masks with none or all pixels marked as errors.
Figure 15:Best-performing model per task×topic across all of our 14 baselines.

Figure 16:Best-performing model per task×topic for closed-source (left) and open-source (middle) models based on human overall scores; theΔ\Deltapanel (right) showsΔ=Closed−Open\Delta=\text{Closed}-\text{Open}.
Figure 17:Average human score per task×topic for closed-source (left) and open-source (middle) models; theΔ\Deltapanel (right) showsΔ=Closed−Open\Delta=\text{Closed}-\text{Open}.
Figure 18:Topic distribution for the entire dataset
Figure 19:Word cloud of subtopics across the entire dataset, with word size proportional to frequency
